name: Green Route 4.3 Polvoranca Park
description: Hike the easy Green Route 4.3 in Polvoranca Park, a 3.8-mile (6.1 km) trail past church ruins and Laguna de María Pascuala.
region_intro: Hiking around Leganés offers accessible outdoor experiences primarily through its green spaces and interconnected park systems. The terrain is generally flat, with trails typically involving less than 1,000 feet (300 meters) of elevation gain, making them suitable for various fitness levels. Notable natural features include the Parque de Polvoranca and Bosquesur, which provide well-maintained paths and botanical gardens. While not characterized by dramatic mountain ranges, Leganés's parks offer a network of routes for local exploration. Best hiking trails around Leganés The most popular hiking route is Green Route 4.3 Polvoranca Park, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This easy circular route explores the varied landscapes within Parque de Polvoranca. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parque Polvoranca — circular, an easy 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Laguna de María Pascuala and passes by church ruins. Local hikers also love the Barranco del Canto Echado y el Parque del Polvoranca — circular desde Fuenlabrada, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through Parque del Polvoranca, often completed in about 2 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Leganés is defined by its green spaces, interconnected park systems, and accessible paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from around 80 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Leganés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Leganés?
A: Leganés offers a wide selection of outdoor routes, with over 80 hiking trails recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more moderate excursions through the region's green spaces.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Leganés hiking trails?
A: The hiking trails in Leganés are generally characterized by flat terrain, primarily through well-maintained park systems and green spaces. Most routes involve less than 300 meters (1,000 feet) of elevation gain, making them suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Leganés?
A: Yes, Leganés is ideal for family-friendly hikes. Many trails are easy and accessible, particularly within Parque de Polvoranca. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957781868/green-route-4-3-polvoranca-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Route 4.3 Polvoranca Park&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.1 km circular path perfect for all ages.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Leganés?
A: Absolutely. Leganés features several popular circular routes, especially within its main parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1354564456/parque-polvoranca-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Polvoranca — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a 6.3 km loop that explores the park's natural features.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Leganés?
A: Many of Leganés's parks and green spaces are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the trails with your canine companion. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Leganés?
A: While hiking in Leganés, you can explore various natural features and landmarks. Parque de Polvoranca, for instance, includes the tranquil Laguna de María Pascuala and interesting church ruins. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, offering a pleasant sight along the trails.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near Leganés that I can visit?
A: Yes, there are a few notable waterfalls you can visit. Within Leganés itself, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Further afield, but still accessible, are the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3440393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retiro’s Crystal Palace and Pond with Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5035208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta de la Fuente del Berro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How can I access the hiking trails in Leganés using public transport?
A: Leganés is well-connected within the Madrid metropolitan area, making many trailheads accessible by public transport. Local bus services often reach the vicinity of major parks like Parque de Polvoranca and Bosquesur, providing convenient access to the trails.

Q: Is parking available near the hiking trailheads in Leganés?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near the main park entrances and trailheads in Leganés, particularly at popular spots like Parque de Polvoranca and Bosquesur. These areas are designed to accommodate visitors arriving by car.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Leganés?
A: The hiking routes in Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Hikers often praise the accessibility of the trails, the well-maintained paths, and the peaceful green spaces that offer a refreshing escape within the urban area.

Q: Are there longer, more challenging hikes that connect multiple parks?
A: For those seeking a longer experience, Leganés offers routes that connect its integrated park systems. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1354805324/polvoranca-park-and-bosquesur-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Park and Bosquesur Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.3 km route that links these two significant green areas, providing a more extended excursion.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Leganés?
A: The best time to hike in Leganés is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summer can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions suitable for walking.
tips: Ruins of the old Hermitage that used to be part of Polvoranca, a village that, according to legend, had a reputation for being cursed and was finally depopulated towards the end of the 19th century.

Nice lake very close to the center of Leganés. It is located within the grounds of the Parque de Polvoranca, a nice place to escape from the city where you can enjoy a variety of paths and paths in good condition to alternate your circuits!

The Polvoranca Park is located across the M-406 and you can easily get there from Leganés or even Alcorcón. It offers everything you can think of to spend a beautiful day, recreational areas, trails and bicycle paths, bird observatory and you cannot miss visiting the María Pascuala Lake. An ideal place to enjoy with the whole family!

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Green Route 4.3 Polvoranca Park?
faqs.answer: This specific Komoot route, the Green Route 4.3, is approximately 6.1 kilometers long and typically takes about 1 hour and 33 minutes to complete at a moderate hiking pace. The park itself offers longer routes if you wish to extend your exploration.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The Green Route 4.3 features well-maintained paths, making it suitable for a relaxed outdoor experience. You'll find a mix of asphalt and wide tracks, ideal for walking and cycling. The route is largely flat, with minimal elevation changes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Green Route 4.3?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied and pleasant landscape. You'll pass by the historic Polvoranca Church Ruins, a baroque-style building from 1655. The trail also leads to the picturesque Laguna de María Pascuala, a central feature of Polvoranca Park. The park also features thematic gardens, an arboretum, and a botanical garden.

faqs.question: Is the Green Route 4.3 Polvoranca Park suitable for families and children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as 'easy' and is very family-friendly. Its largely flat and well-maintained paths make it accessible for a wide range of visitors, including those with strollers or reduced mobility. The park's diverse scenery and educational center offer additional appeal for families.

faqs.question: Is the Green Route 4.3 Polvoranca Park dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Polvoranca Park is generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect the park's rich biodiversity and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The park's diverse flora and fauna make it enjoyable year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. However, the well-maintained paths mean it's accessible even in milder winter days or early summer mornings/evenings.

faqs.question: Are there any entrance fees or permits required for Polvoranca Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or special permits required to access Polvoranca Park or hike the Green Route 4.3. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: How can I get to Polvoranca Park using public transport?
faqs.answer: Polvoranca Park is highly accessible and well-connected to public transport, including Cercanías train stations. You can check local transport schedules for the best route to the park from your location.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails in Polvoranca Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the Green Route 4.3 covers 94% of the official 'Ruta Verde 4.3 Parque Polvoranca', it also briefly intersects with sections of other trails. These include parts of the 'Ruta Verde 4.4 Fuenlabrada - Parque Polvoranca - Alcorcón' and various 'Circuito de Salud' paths within the park.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or restrooms available in Polvoranca Park?
faqs.answer: Polvoranca Park is a significant green space with facilities. While specific cafes directly on the Green Route 4.3 are not detailed, the park hosts an Environmental Education Center which may have facilities, and being a hub for surrounding municipalities, amenities are available within the wider park area or nearby towns.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: Polvoranca Park is recognized for its ecological richness, providing a safe habitat for various flora and fauna. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, you might spot a variety of birds, small mammals, and insects, especially around the lagoons and forested areas. The botanical garden and arboretum also showcase diverse plant species.
